figures.caption: Table 1: The averaged PMSEs for the Los Angeles homeless data under different trainingtest split ratios, with the corresponding standard errors reported in parentheses.
Table 2: The estimated coefficient vector for the Los Angeles homeless data under different training-test split ratios.

Figure 3: Relative OMSE for labeled sample size n = 50 and 200 with varying R2, based on 500 Monte Carlo replicates, under Case I and II when the correctly specified model is included among the candidates (i.e., α = 0).
Figure 4: Relative OMSE for R2 = 0.5 with varying n, based on 500 Monte Carlo replicates, under Case I and II with α = 0.1 and α = 0, respectively.

Figure 1: A schematic diagram of the construction of candidate strategies. In addition to the candidate models, the set {λ1, λ2, . . . , λS2} denotes the power tuning parameters, and {f1, f2, . . . , fS3} denotes the pre-trained ML algorithms. These components are repeatedly combined across different candidate models. For example, the red arrows illustrate a possible construction process of candidate strategy m, which is formed by combining the s1th candidate model, the s2th power tuning parameter, and the s3th ML algorithm.
Figure 2: Relative OMSE for labeled sample size n = 50 and 200 with varying R2, based on 500 Monte Carlo replicates, under Case I and II when all candidate models are misspecified (i.e., α = 0.1).
